title: MiniPay cron routes have no direct route coverage
reasoning: The new MiniPay routes carry several production-sensitive contracts: bearer-only cron auth, missing-env failures, Dune auth mapping, cursor advancement after successful page writes, dryRun skipping writes, limit handling, existing-label filtering, and empty-set no-op behavior. The supplied tests cover analogous Arkham cron auth/config/pipeline behavior, but none import or exercise either MiniPay route, so regressions in these cron-only paths would not be caught by the linked route suites.
recommendation: Add dedicated Vitest route tests for /api/minipay/sync and /api/minipay/tag following the Arkham cron route test shape: mock requireCronAuth/minipay helpers/discovery/labels/Sentry, then assert auth/config errors, happy-path bodies, dryRun behavior, no writes on empty set, and cursor advancement only after successful page writes.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: All included tests target address-labels, address-reports, Arkham, and local Hasura routes. None of them import ui-dashboard/src/app/api/minipay/sync/route.ts or ui-dashboard/src/app/api/minipay/tag/route.ts.
suggestedRegressionTest: Create ui-dashboard/src/app/api/minipay/__tests__/route.test.ts with separate describe blocks for sync and tag; include a sync test where addToMiniPaySet rejects mid-stream and setLastSyncedBlock is not called, and a tag dryRun test where importLabels is not called but wouldWrite is returned.
minimumFixScope: Add MiniPay route tests under ui-dashboard/src/app/api/minipay/**/__tests__ or a shared minipay route test file; no production code change is required for this finding.

history.note: MiniPay sync/tag route tests now cover cron auth/config/happy/no-op paths; focused route tests pass.
